tweets.text:
Observation: Teams are spending 40%+ of their token budget just re-establishing context that agents forgot from the last session.

The problem isn't the model. It's that agent memory has no persistence layer.

We built Memoria — version control for AI agent memory. Think Git, but https://t.co/FcUa0E2ZK7

tweets.text:
Personal agents may create a new kind of network effect.

Not just users, not just data, but context that compounds around a person or team.

If that proves true, markets will start watching identity, permissions, and agent memory as core infra metrics.

#PredictionMarkets

tweets.text:
I think wearables are about to become agent memory.

Not just charts. Not just dashboards.

Sleep, HRV, heart rate and activity should be private context for AI assistants.

So I built Fitbit MCP.

Unofficial. Open-source. Local-first.

https://t.co/Nqpe2GYGK1
tweets.name: David Batista

tweets.text:
brain vs memory vs session framing from @garrytan's gbrain:

- (g)brain: world knowledge (people, companies, meetings)
- agent memory: operational state (preferences, decisions, config)
- session context: what was just said

useful frame whether you use gbrain or not
tweets.name: 0x471

tweets.text:
Intelligence is cheap; context is expensive. @memoria_ai is right — agent memory is the missing layer. 

A model without history is just a transient thought. The ultimate leverage in the AI age is building the persistence layer for digital labor.

tweets.text:
Agent memory is the shift from stateless to persistent. When your AI knows your network—not just facts but relationships—context compounds across sessions. Every interaction builds on the last.

Memory is not storage. Memory is continuity.

tweets.name: Helix

tweets.text: Most agent memory systems lose the plot after a few sessions. ZenBrain hits 91.3% of a long-context oracle's accuracy on LongMemEval-500 — at 1/106 the token cost. Beats Letta, Mem0, A-Mem in 12/12 head-to-head tests (Bonferroni-corrected). 7 layers. Open source. https://t.co/nqMGlsF8Mc
tweets.name: ZenSation AI

tweets.text: The infrastructure question everyone skips: where does agent memory actually live? Persistent memory running for days sounds great until you realize it's vendor-controlled, rate-limited, and context-window constrained. The real unlock is local-first knowledge graphs—traversable,
tweets.name: Helix

tweets.text: The race to own AI agent memory is heating up. Obsidian vault as persistent memory for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini—local-first, structured, traversable. This is exactly the architecture that wins: your notes become nodes, relationships become queries, and context compounds instead
tweets.name: Helix

Most agent memory systems optimize for storage.
But production agents need relevance over time.

Memory Decay is our first step toward making memory more time-aware. This matters a lot for long-running agents.

The longer memory grows, the more old context competes with current https://t.co/MDznQ8Ve5Z

tweets.text:
anthropic just shipped “sleep” into agents.

humans don’t just store yesterday, we replay it fast, extract patterns, then rewrite it into long-term memory.

most agent “memory” rn is just a search engine: embed, retrieve, paste back into context. good for facts. zero compounding. https://t.co/bbt566W2Q8

Also shipped today: per-recall outcome attribution for our agent memory system. Every retrieval logs (memory_id, query_embedding); a LLM judges outcome; future recalls get a cosine²-weighted bonus from past
outcomes in similar contexts.

tweets.name: fasrad

tweets.text:
Your AI agent is not forgetting. It is remembering too much

Every old rule you gave it still competes with the new one.

Every stale preference becomes fake context

Every half-correct workflow stays alive longer than it should

This is why agent memory starts feeling "smart" at https://t.co/lNaBY7yXtB https://t.co/Ysza3y4N3E

tweets.text:
你 Claude / Codex 一个月烧 $200+ token 的元凶不是模型贵，是没 memory。

每次新会话从 0 开始解释项目背景 → 70% token 烧在重复 context。

调研了一圈 agent memory 层，整理出 4 派系 18 个项目 — 跨会话记得住、跨 agent 共享、token 直降一半 🧵 https://t.co/Vo17gp1PeG
tweets.name: Jason Zhu

tweets.text:
外部记忆不是持续学习的免死金牌。

arXiv 2604.27003 这个结论很刺耳：把经验丢进 memory，并没有消灭稳定-可塑性困境，只是把战场从参数更新挪到了 retrieval。

老经验和新经验会在有限 context 里抢位置；抽象程序记忆比完整轨迹更能迁移；负迁移还专门伤害 hard cases。

所以 agent memory https://t.co/FADFNoRoWc
